perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Hydron shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon written notice to Dolphins at any time between November 1, 1997 and December 15, 1997, in which event this Agreement shall be terminated following the Dolphins last 1997 regular or post-season game, if any. If Hydron does not timely exercise its right to terminate this Agreement, then Hydron agrees that this Agreement shall continue for the entire four (4) year term unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 8 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Hydron shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon written notice to Dolphins at any time between November 1, 1997 and December 15, 1997, in which event this Agreement shall be terminated following the Dolphins last 1997 regular or post-season game, if any. If Hydron does not timely exercise its right to terminate this Agreement, then Hydron agrees that this Agreement shall continue for the entire four (4) year term unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 8 or Section 1.2(a) of this Agreement. 1.2(a) Hydron can terminate this agreement, with a 30 day written notice, with no financial penalty.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the agreement continues for four years unless terminated under Section 8. The added clause 1.2(a) gives Hydron another way to terminate the agreement (30-day notice, no penalty), creating uncertainty about the conditions for Hydron's ability to terminate and contradicting the original terms of 1.2 that mention that after a specific time period, they have to continue the agreement for the entire (4) year term unless Section 8 is in play. This would give Hydron the chance to terminate whenever, defeating the purpose of section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Hydron shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon written notice to Dolphins at any time between November 1, 1997 and December 15, 1997, in which event this Agreement shall be terminated following the Dolphins last 1997 regular or post-season game, if any. If Hydron does not timely exercise its right to terminate this Agreement, then Hydron agrees that this Agreement shall continue for the entire four (4) year term unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 8 or Section 1.2(a) of this Agreement. 1.2(a) Hydron can terminate this agreement, with a 30 day written notice, with no financial penalty.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement stipulates a four-year term unless terminated under Section 8, but the modified agreement introduces Section 1.2(a), allowing Hydron to terminate with a 30-day notice without financial penalty. This directly contradicts the original four-year commitment and effectively nullifies the limitations outlined in the original Section 1.2 regarding the timing of Hydron's ability to terminate (November 1, 1997 and December 15, 1997). The contradicted text, also Section 1.2 in this case, is what's being contradicted, as the addition of 1.2(a) creates a clear inconsistency within the contract regarding termination conditions, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 In the event that the consideration is not paid by Hydron on or before the applicable payment due date, said failure to pay shall be considered a material breach by Hydron, and the Dolphins may elect to charge Hydron a late fee of 1.5% per month of the payment then due and owing until it is paid in full. The Dolphins agree to provide written notice to Hydron of the failure to receive any payment, and Hydron shall have a five (5) business day period following delivery of written notice in which to cure the payment default before the Dolphins may elect to terminate this Agreement and pursue applicable remedies. It is agreed by Hydron that any such election of remedies does not waive any other remedies for breach of contract available to the Dolphins.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 In the event that the consideration is not paid by Hydron on or before the applicable payment due date, said failure to pay shall be considered a material breach by Hydron, and the Dolphins may elect to charge Hydron a late fee of 1.5% per month of the payment then due and owing until it is paid in full. However, late fees will not apply to the first instance of late payment. The Dolphins agree to provide written notice to Hydron of the failure to receive any payment, and Hydron shall have a five (5) business day period following delivery of written notice in which to cure the payment default before the Dolphins may elect to terminate this Agreement and pursue applicable remedies. It is agreed by Hydron that any such election of remedies does not waive any other remedies for breach of contract available to the Dolphins.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction because it first states that late fees *may* be charged on any late payment, but then adds that late fees *will not* apply to the first late payment. This provides contradictory information about when late fees can be applied, making the enforcement of the original clause uncertain. The original terms of 3.2 have the potential to be made useless, due to the newly added sentence, indicating that there is no late fee on the first payment, which goes against the purpose of the terms in 3.2 overall.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 In the event that the consideration is not paid by Hydron on or before the applicable payment due date, said failure to pay shall be considered a material breach by Hydron, and the Dolphins may elect to charge Hydron a late fee of 1.5% per month of the payment then due and owing until it is paid in full. However, late fees will not apply to the first instance of late payment. The Dolphins agree to provide written notice to Hydron of the failure to receive any payment, and Hydron shall have a five (5) business day period following delivery of written notice in which to cure the payment default before the Dolphins may elect to terminate this Agreement and pursue applicable remedies. It is agreed by Hydron that any such election of remedies does not waive any other remedies for breach of contract available to the Dolphins.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction within section 3.2. The original text of 3.2 states that late fees 'may' be charged on any late payment. The changed text adds 'However, late fees will not apply to the first instance of late payment.' This direct addition creates an inconsistency in the text, stating that late fees may be charged but also that they will not be charged for the first instance. This creates a strong contradiction, making the enforcement of late fees unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 9.6 No party may assign any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, except that Hydron may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to its parent, its successor or to an affiliate (as such term is defined under the rules and regulations promulgated under the federal securities laws of the U.S.) upon the reasonable consent of the Dolphins that such affiliate assignee has the financial means and corporate authority to perform such obligations and Hydron may not withhold its consent to an assignment of this Agreement in the event of a merger or reorganization of the Dolphins, a sale of all or substantially all of the Dolphins' assets or a consolidation of the Dolphins with any of its affiliates or related parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.6 No party may assign any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, except that Hydron may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to its parent, its successor or to an affiliate (as such term is defined under the rules and regulations promulgated under the federal securities laws of the U.S.) without any consent needed of the Dolphins and Hydron may not withhold its consent to an assignment of this Agreement in the event of a merger or reorganization of the Dolphins, a sale of all or substantially all of the Dolphins' assets or a consolidation of the Dolphins with any of its affiliates or related parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, Hydron could assign rights to its parent, successor, or affiliate with reasonable consent from the Dolphins. However, the modified text removes the condition of 'reasonable consent' of the Dolphins, while in another line, keeping that Hydron has no right to withhold concent for the Dolphins' business reasons. This creates an uncertainty about whether or not the Dolphins have power to negotiate/consent on Hydron's assigns, while Hydron has no ability to affect the assigns of the Dolphins. This favors one party over another creating an imbalance in the agreement's terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.6 No party may assign any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, except that Hydron may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to its parent, its successor or to an affiliate (as such term is defined under the rules and regulations promulgated under the federal securities laws of the U.S.) without any consent needed of the Dolphins and Hydron may not withhold its consent to an assignment of this Agreement in the event of a merger or reorganization of the Dolphins, a sale of all or substantially all of the Dolphins' assets or a consolidation of the Dolphins with any of its affiliates or related parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Dolphins' 'reasonable consent' for Hydron to assign its rights to its parent, successor, or affiliate. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the same section (and the contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text in this case) which states, 'No party may assign any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party'. By removing the Dolphins' consent requirement for Hydron's assignments but keeping the restriction on assignments without consent in place, the document creates an inconsistency and imbalance, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
